Corrected 6 item labels from flattened dot-notation to the form's own double-paren notation (root cause RC-4, confirmed cosmetic-format defect in the audit but not previously fixed): 1.a.1.a.(1)/(2)->1.a.(1)(a)(1)/(2) (RIAD4435/4436), 1.a.1.d.(1)/(2)->1.a.(1)(d)(1)/(2) (RIADB485/B486), 2.a.1.b.(1)/(2)/(3)->2.a.(1)(b)(1)/(2)/(3) (RIAD0093/HK03/HK04), M.13.a.1->M.13.a.(1) (RIADF552), M.13.b.1->M.13.b.(1) (RIADF554). 21 further legacy rows (order 91-111) correctly item:null, not enumerated. Item 10 ('sequence_exclusions.RCO["10"]' claims a gap) confirmed populated and correct (RCFDK656, banker's bank certification) -- that exclusions-doc staleness lives in a different file, not an issue here. AMBIGUOUS, left unresolved per mandate -- Memoranda item 18 (PDF p.55-56, a 15-column PD-bucket table for rows a-j): row M.18.a is entirely absent (audit found RCFDM964-M978 all item:null in the tree) and rows M.18.b-M.18.j each carry only a single code (RCFDM993/N009/N024/N054/N069/N084/N099/N114/N128) where the form's own column structure implies up to 13 further dollar-amount PD-bucket columns per row plus a methodology-flag column. The audit's own Part-B reviewer flagged this as 'NEEDS FOLLOW-UP, not confirmed' -- unable to determine from PDF text alone (no panel-data access) whether the missing columns are a genuine structural gap or legitimate no-filer-data suppression (the completeness-exclusions file's own no-data list for this MDRM range is independently known to be stale elsewhere, per the audit, undermining it as a tiebreaker). Cannot confidently resolve without panel verification -- leaving PARTIAL, derived_from_tree retained, rather than guessing the missing column codes/order. Item list above independently cross-checked against the form for everything except M.18's column completeness. ~2700 further rows (order 73+) correctly item:null legacy/historical. CYCLE-11.1 STEP-2 ADJUDICATION of M.18 (rendered images c111_step2_call_p55/p56.png, read directly): the form prints a 15-column PD grid (Columns A-N amounts: <=1% through Unscoreable + Total; Column O 'PDs Were Derived Using' number; row j Total has no Column O) for rows a-j: a=M964-M978, b=M979-M993, c=M994-N009, d=N010-N024, e=N040-N054, f=N055-N069, g=N070-N084, h=N085-N099, i=N100-N114, j=N115-N128. duckdb panel proof: EVERY M.18 code (RCFD and RCON variants, 125 codes checked) has ZERO rows in the public CDR panel - the form itself states amounts 'will not be made available to the public on an individual institution basis' (confidential item). Adjudication: NOT a tree gap - public-data suppression. The tree keeps one representative row per form row (b-i carry the Column O methodology code M993/N009/N024/ N054/N069/N084/N099/N114; j carries its Column N total N128), all correctly grey (zero data); M.18.a stays a documented reserved gap (its 15 codes incl. M978 were never tree-homed and adding them would only add grey rows and disturb the banked EMPTY_CODES 662). Row M.18.a's absence and the 13-per-row column exclusions are hereby the documented form-vs-tree deltas. Status PROMOTED to COMPLETE.

Regulatory Capital Components and Ratios') via full-row audit (hierarchy_audit_call_B.md, pdftotext-sourced) -- RCRI is the fullest/most form-complete of the three RC-R-Part-I keys. Both prior item-collision defects confirmed fixed: RCOAJJ29 (CECL transition election question) correctly at 2.a (was colliding bare '2' with parent RCFAKW00), RCOAP838 (AOCI opt-out election) correctly at 3.a (was colliding bare '3' with parent RCFAB530). Item 43 confirmed a genuine gap on the form (sequence correctly jumps 42/42.b -> 44/44.b, not a defect). Items 56.a/56.b (Leverage buffer standard / Leverage buffer, RCFALE89/RCFALE90) are correctly absent here: confirmed absent from both the hierarchy JSON and ffiec_call_dictionary.csv entirely (not merely unmapped -- the MDRM codes have no dictionary entry at all), consistent with an upstream MDRM-dictionary-pull gap predating this 06/2026 form revision; per the project's no-empty-chart-silently constraint these must not be wired in until panel-data availability is confirmed, so their absence here is the correct, deliberate disposition, not a gap needing a guess. Items 4-55 read through with no further anomalies found beyond the two fixed collisions. REDUNDANCY CLAIM CONFIRMED: the form's own TOC and body (p.61-65) show exactly one 'Part I' section with no 'Part I-A'/'Part I-B' sub-labeling anywhere -- RCRI alone reflects it; see RCRIA/RCRIB entries for confirmation their content is 100% either inert or a strict-subset duplicate of RCRI's own (already form-true) rows. CYCLE-12 STEP-2 PAGE VERIFICATION (rendered images c12_step2_call_p62/p64/p65.png, read directly): all 9 multi-column items verified form-true in order - 12/17/18/19 dual RCFA|RCFW (p.62; item 11 = RCFWP851 single Column-B box, matching the Y-9C HC-R item-11 adjudication class, already correct); 33 = KX77|KX78 and 34.d = KX82|KX83 dollar|percentage pairs (p.64); 49/50/51 = P793/7206/7205 RCFA|RCFW ratio pairs (p.65).

An empty items array is therefore the form-true state for this key (zero genuine, independently-sourced numbered form items belong here); confirmed the contamination row and all other previously-populated rows are now correctly item:null in the source. Every force_row's item/depth was cross-checked against an already-correctly-tagged sibling column in the same row (matching the codebase's own native depth convention: depth=1 for unlettered items, depth=2 for lettered a/b/c sub-items) -- not guessed. Items 26/27/28-31 and ALL of Memoranda M.1/M.2.a-g/M.3.a-g/M.4.a-c were independently verified ALREADY CORRECTLY TAGGED in the base hierarchy (native, pre-override) -- zero changes needed there, confirmed via direct grep, not assumed. Item 6 (RCFD3123) needed no fix -- form prints the identical code for both columns A and B. RCFD1773's pre-existing item-2.b force_row (from an earlier, pre-cycle-11 fix cycle) was left untouched per the ledger's explicit note that it is 'not a new finding' -- out of this crosswalk's scope. Zero codes excluded/unplaced -- every PDF-derived code for items 1-23+25 was located in the base array and matched to its form cell with a caption-text cross-check. Result: this is now the largest single-pass crosswalk in the project (266 codes) and moves RCRII from the audit's 'CRITICAL, ~88pct item:null' status to fully form-true. CYCLE-11.1 STEP-2 RE-VERIFICATION (rendered form-page images, pdftoppm-class evidence c111_step2_call_p66..p78.png + geometric x-sorted code extraction validated against direct image reads; full per-item column sequences in c111_step2_form_column_sequences.json): items 20-23 re-verified code-by-code as tasked; additionally ALL items 1-25 column sequences were extracted and 17 anchor mdrm entries corrected to the form's LAST printed column code (this manifest's anchor convention: the checker compares the manifest mdrm to the LAST tree row sharing the item string, so the anchor must be the form's last column - e.g. item 20's last column is Column S RCFDH310 on p.76, not Column J RCFDS548; item 11's is RCFDH300; item 8's page-2 continuation ends at RCFDH295). Items 4 and 24 are printed header/factor rows with no code boxes (pp.66,77-78) - now expected as blank-mdrm rows. RCFD1773 remains an out-of-scope pre-existing extra row on item 2.b (ledger-noted, checker-invisible). CYCLE-12 STEP-0 (rendered image c12_step0_call_p79.png, read directly): Memoranda column grids populated (M.1=G642; M.2.a-g=S582-S602 and M.3.a-g=S603-S623 as 3-column maturity grids; M.4.a-c=JJ30-32). ADJUDICATION: the ledger-protected extra RCFD1773 row on item 2.b is REMOVED from this schedule - the form's 14 printed columns (pp.66-67, verified cycle-11.1) do not include it; the code keeps its form-verified home at RC 2.b, so nothing leaves the dashboard. The cycle-11 'out of scope' deferral is hereby closed.

RCT.notes: Independently verified against form PDF p.83-86 (RC-68..RC-71) via full-row audit (hierarchy_audit_call_B.md, pdftotext-sourced). Both prior defects confirmed fixed and linked as the audit described: RIAD4070 (Total gross fiduciary and related services income) correctly at item 22, no longer squatting on the false item-5 slot (item 5 is a header only, RIAD4070 does not appear there); RIADB912 (fiduciary income, foreign offices) correctly at 22.a, not standing in for the parent total. Item 8 (Foundation and endowment trust and agency accounts, RCFDJ255/J256/J257/J258) confirmed present with all 4 columns correctly sharing bare item '8' as siblings -- resolves a prior open question, this was never a real gap; it simply lacks the synthetic-header-plus-lettered-children wrapper that items 4/5.a-c/6/7/9/10 use for the same 4-column pattern (both approaches are confirmed internally consistent/deliberate per the audit, this is a cosmetic-only visual difference, not corrected here). Item 11 correctly has only .b/.d (no Managed-assets/# columns for custody-and-safekeeping, matching the form's own omission). Items 14-21 (fiduciary income by account type) and M.1-M.4 (collective investment fund detail, losses) read through with no anomalies. Large block of RCON domestic-office duplicates and pre-2001 legacy variants correctly excluded (item:null), not enumerated. CYCLE-11.1 STEP-5 (31 anchor fixes; rendered images c111_step5_call_p84/p85/p86.png, read directly): the Memoranda print as multi-column grids - M.1.a-p = 3 columns (Personal Trust/Employee Benefit/All Other, e.g. M.1.j = J290/J291/J292), M.1.q = J311/J312, M.2.a = B927/B928, M.2.a.(1) = J313/J314, M.2.b = B929, M.3.a-h = Number/Market-Value pairs (B931-B946), M.4.a-e = RIAD 3-column loss grids (B947-B961). Anchors corrected from the Column-A code to the LAST printed column code per the checker's last-one-wins convention. CYCLE-12 STEP-0: explicit columns for all 33 multi-column Memoranda items from the pp.84-86 renders read in cycle-11.1 (M.1.a-p 3-column trust grids, M.1.q/M.2.a/M.2.a.(1)/M.3.a-h pairs, M.4.a-e RIAD loss triplets). CYCLE-12 STEP-2 NOTE: item 12's four RCFN columns (B900-B903, p.84) are tree-homed as sub-items 12.a-12.d (audited convention) - NOT orphaned; a mid-cycle orphan-restore attempt was reverted after the reachability check showed them itemized.
